# devhelm_secret (Resource)

Manages a DevHelm vault secret for use in monitor authentication.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Secrets are referenced by `key` from monitor `auth` blocks via the
# `{{ secret.<key> }}` interpolation syntax. The value is write-only — the
# API never returns it, and the provider tracks `value_hash` to detect drift
# without ever pulling the plaintext back into state.
resource "devhelm_secret" "api_token" {
  key   = "api_token"
  value = var.api_token
}

resource "devhelm_secret" "github_pat" {
  key   = "github_pat"
  value = var.github_pat
}

# Reference a secret from a monitor.
resource "devhelm_monitor" "api" {
  name              = "API"
  type              = "HTTP"
  frequency_seconds = 60
  config            = jsonencode({ url = "https://api.example.com/health", method = "GET" })
  auth = jsonencode({
    type  = "bearer"
    token = "{{ secret.${devhelm_secret.api_token.key} }}"
  })
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `key` (String) Secret key (used to reference this secret in auth configs)
- `value` (String, Sensitive) Secret value (write-only, never returned by the API)

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Unique identifier for this secret
- `value_hash` (String) SHA-256 hash of the secret value (used for drift detection)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Secrets are imported by their key (the value is never re-fetched).
terraform import devhelm_secret.api_token api_token
```
